indentation-and-blocks.label: Indentation and Blocks
indentation-and-blocks.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

quiz1.title: What does indentation control in Python?
quiz1.prompt: Choose the correct statement about indentation.
quiz1.hint: Think about how Python interprets code structure.

help.concept: Indentation in Python defines the grouping of statements. It is essential for the correct execution of control flow.
help.hint_1: Indentation is not just for aesthetics; it has functional importance.
help.hint_2: Consider how blocks of code are formed in other programming languages.

options.a: It controls the execution speed of the program.
options.b: It defines the structure of code blocks.
options.c: It is optional and can be ignored.
options.d: It is used only for comments.

quiz2.title: Which of the following statements about blocks in Python are true?
quiz2.hint: Focus on the main concept being tested and eliminate choices that describe something different.

help.concept: Choose the option that matches the core idea described in the question without relying on repeated wording from the answer choices.
help.hint_1: Rule out options that describe a different role, behavior, or concept than the question is testing.
help.hint_2: Pick the option that best matches the concept, not the one that simply repeats familiar wording.

options.b: Each block must start with a new indentation level.
options.c: Blocks can be nested within other blocks.
options.d: Indentation can be mixed with tabs and spaces.

quiz3.prompt: In Python, indentation is used to define a ______.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind complete the statement about indentation..
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.

quiz4.title: Create a simple if-else statement.
quiz4.prompt: Write a program that checks if a number is positive or negative.

sketch1.title: Understanding Indentation
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of the code. Blocks of code are defined by their indentation level.

sketch2.title: Blocks and Control Flow
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: Control flow statements like if, elif, and else rely on indentation to determine which statements belong to which block.
